title: Postpartum mood follow-up

red_flags.text: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports clinician-completed mental-health risk assessment for postpartum mood follow-up; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.

source.title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations

examination.text: Requires maternal physical and emotional assessment, postpartum haemorrhage and hypertensive-risk screening, feeding support, family planning discussion, continuity, and EMR documentation.
examination.rationale: Supports postnatal emotional-health assessment, education, support and continuity for postpartum mood follow-up; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Requires maternal physical and emotional assessment, postpartum haemorrhage and hypertensive-risk screening, feeding support, family planning discussion, continuity, and EMR documentation.

exact_section.heading: 3.1 Core Components — maternal physical and emotional health, education, support, and continuity

examination.text: Specifies vital signs, uterine involution, lochia, wound healing, bladder and bowel function, feeding, mood, support, family planning, pelvic floor, indicated tests, and documented follow-up.
examination.rationale: Supports detailed postnatal maternal assessment for postpartum mood follow-up; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Specifies vital signs, uterine involution, lochia, wound healing, bladder and bowel function, feeding, mood, support, family planning, pelvic floor, indicated tests, and documented follow-up.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Postpartum timing, mood course, anxiety, sleep, bonding, feeding, supports, psychosis or mania assessment, risk, diagnosis and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for postpartum mood follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
